title: What an American thinks about Singapore

transcript.text: What an american thinks about singapore this american thinks that singapore
Is a nice place it's very clean I was there in 1999 for like a couple days
And uh it's very clean you know very first world country feel
Malaysia a lot of malaysia has that first world country feel too
Singapore was part of malaysia until like I don't know 1960 or something
Um singapore is that place along with malaysia that gives you the death penalty for drugs so
And I think there's a really big fine like the thurian prison for spitting on the sidewalk and
Stuff so but uh it's a little island the whole country is a little island and uh the whole thing
Is inhabited so it's really hard to find um like the center of the city if you're if you're at
Night and you can't see the um the uh the uh skyscrapers um it's been all night trying to
Find it one night so I was like where's the city gonna start the whole island is a city
Um but the downtown area is down on the southern side of it um they have an indian section with
People dressed in indian garb and like a chinese section and stuff so it's like it's like the
Melting pot of all the different races of uh of uh south asia southeast asia
Um it uh has a nice looking um downtown area with all the skyscrapers you know like a first world
Country I got a hotel and I had a tv in the hotel and their news was in english so their first country
Their national country that language is english so it's weird it's like another america you know
Except they kill you if they find you catching a smoking joint I guess an australian national
Kid some kid was executed for buying like an ounce of heroin over there but um I think my hotel was
Like seven eight bucks it wasn't that much um a lot of like expats there too I went to some restaurant
Late one night and had a margarita and it was like a you know it's like in america it was like a
Outback restaurant or something so the culture is really western I took the bus around the day
One day just to look at the look at the town for some reason I didn't want to ride my bike I just
Took the bus and um it's um it's really populated like that whole island is a city like if you wanted
To consider it singapore city it's probably up there and one of the biggest cities um and uh
Yeah it looked a lot like america I didn't interact with a whole lot of people there I parked my bike
Um to do the tour of some like section of the island maybe like an island off the coast of it or
Something on the southern end where the uh battlements were where the forts were from like
A long time ago like the english or whoever were there and somebody uh deflated all the air out of
My mountain bike tire because I parked it I locked it to a uh pole a metal pole um fence it's only
Happened twice in my life but um you can see singapore from batam this island in indonesia
That's just like a few miles south and indonesia is like really poor those people can't just go to
Singapore they're like it's like india looking across the island at like england or something it's
Yeah you can see all the skyscrapers it must be weird for those super because I went into indonesia
They're poor like they're these ladies they wanted me to stay with them at their house
And they were like really poor man on the side of this mountain on this hill and they had like their
House was just like toothpicks you know but um yeah I looked over and you can see singapore
All the skyscrapers um
So uh yeah that's what I think about singapore look look defluent to me I don't know I don't think
They make that much but yeah okay
